name: Back up key records with Airtable and Google Drive in real time

parameters.text: =🛑 Tonight's backup came out EMPTY — investigate immediately.
=✅ Back up key records with Airtable and Google Drive in real time — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Hourly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 1 h. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Export All Rows (Google Sheets)

### Export All Rows (Google Sheets)

Reads every row from 'Record Tracker' — the read side of the store, e.g. for a digest or a downstream sync.

Ports: ①→ Serialize to CSV · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Serialize to CSV

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Compress Archive

### Compress Archive

Zips the binary to cut storage/transfer size before upload.

Next: Upload Backup to Google Drive

### Upload Backup to Google Drive

Uploads the generated file to Google Drive as a timestamped archive copy.

Ports: ①→ Backup Size Sane? · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Upload Backup to Google Drive

parameters.content:
### Backup Size Sane?

Two-way gate on `={{ ($binary.data?.fileSize ?? 1) > 0 }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Fingerprint for Idempotency · ②→ Alarm — Empty Backup

### Alarm — Empty Backup

Sends a Microsoft Teams notification via the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per record (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

Searches Airtable's 'Records' table by formula (`={LastModified} > DATEADD(NOW(), -1, 'days')`) for records the pipeline hasn't handled yet.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Copy to Google Drive

### Archive Copy to Google Drive

Uploads the generated file to Google Drive as a timestamped archive copy.

Ports: ①→ Stamp Completion Time · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Archive Copy to Google Drive

parameters.content:
### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

Appends a new row to 'Record Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#data-ops`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

### Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

parameters.content:
### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

Appends a new row to 'Record Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

# Back up key records with Airtable and Google Drive in real time

**Pattern:** Backup & Archive · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Back up key records with Airtable and Google Drive in real time. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual etl is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Hourly Schedule → Export All Rows (Google Sheets) → Serialize to CSV → Compress Archive → Upload Backup to Google Drive → Backup Size Sane? → Fingerprint for Idempotency → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to backup & archive without manual effort
- Anyone automating etl in data
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Responds in seconds
- Standardizes the process every time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Airtable PAT; Google OAuth2; Google OAuth2 / service account; Microsoft OAuth2; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The empty-backup check exists because a backup that silently 'succeeds' with zero rows is worse than an obvious failure — it gives false confidence right up until the day you actually need to restore. Compression runs before upload, not after, cutting both transfer time and storage footprint in the same step.

## Practice exercises

Already production-grade — deepen your understanding:

1. Point the export at an empty table on purpose and confirm the alarm fires.
2. Turn off compression and compare the resulting backup file size.
3. Add a retention step that deletes backups older than N days from the destination.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your etl process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
